title: NOC 72013 – Contractors and supervisors, carpentry trades | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Carpentry Contractors and Supervisors (NOC 72013) are the vital link between high-level architectural design and the hands-on execution of Canada’s housing and infrastructure goals. As the construction industry pivots toward Mass Timber and High-Performance Building Envelopes, these supervisors are responsible for coordinating teams of carpenters and cabinetmakers on complex residential and commercial sites. Operating at the TEER 2 level, they combine master-level carpentry skills with modern project management to ensure structural integrity and compliance with the 2026 National Building Code’s enhanced energy-efficiency standards.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Usually requires college, apprenticeship, or supervisory experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group720 – Technical trades contractors and supervisors

duties: Project Coordination: Supervising, coordinating, and scheduling the activities of carpenters and cabinetmakers across multiple work sites.
Technical Quality Control: Establishing methods to meet work schedules and coordinating activities with other sub-trades (plumbing, electrical) to ensure seamless integration.
Resource Logistics: Requisitioning materials and supplies and resolving work-flow problems to improve site productivity.
Training & Mentorship: Training or arranging for the training of apprentices and ensuring that all workers follow 2026 Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) standards.
Administrative Reporting: Preparing progress reports, materials estimates, and recommending personnel actions such as hirings or promotions.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Experience: Several years of experience as a qualified Journeyperson Carpenter or Cabinetmaker are mandatory.
Certification: Journeyperson trade certification in carpentry or cabinetmaking is usually required.
Professional Skills: Proficiency in blueprint reading, CADD software, and advanced knowledge of computerized maintenance management systems (CMMS).
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Moderate to Strong, specifically driven by the National Housing Strategy and a critical shortage of mid-level management in the trades:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. On February 18, 2026, Ontario issued a targeted OINP draw specifically for NOC 72013 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream. The minimum score required was 50, signaling a high priority for carpentry supervisors to lead the province's residential expansion.

Alberta: Strong demand. Supervisors in Alberta command some of the highest salaries in the country, with median annual earnings reaching $87,880, supported by robust industrial and residential growth in Edmonton and Calgary.

British Columbia: Moderate prospects. While general carpentry is seasonal, supervisors are in steady demand for high-end custom builds and "Multiplex" projects. Approximately 40% of workers in this NOC in BC are self-employed contractors.

National Trend: Over 68% of job vacancies in the construction sector through 2033 will arise from the need to replace retiring workers. This creates a significant "leadership vacuum" that favors international supervisors with documented experience.
Why Work in Canada as a Carpentry Supervisor?
Lead the Prefab Revolution: Gain expertise in 2026-grade technologies, including Modular Construction and Mass Timber assembly (CLT/DLT), which are reshaping the Canadian skyline.
Entrepreneurial Advantage: This NOC has one of the highest rates of self-employment (nearly 40% in some provinces), allowing you to manage your own company and hire your own crews.
High-Tier Trades Pay: As a TEER 2 professional, your earning potential far exceeds general site work, with experienced supervisors in major cities earning up to $106,000+.
Priority for PR: In early 2026, this NOC has been a consistent target for Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) "Skilled Trades" draws, offering a clear path to Permanent Residency.
pathways: Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): On February 18, 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72013 with a score of 50+ under the Foreign Worker stream. This is a prime opportunity for those with an Ontario job offer.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A newly confirmed federal initiative for 2026–2027 targeting 33,000 temporary workers. Carpentry supervisors are classified as "High-Priority Construction Workers."
Express Entry (Federal Skilled Trades): As a TEER 2 technical trade, it is eligible for category-based draws for skilled trades, which typically have much lower CRS requirements than general draws.
BC PNP Construction Stream: A reliable route for supervisors with a job offer from a BC-based carpentry or general contracting firm.
